title: Veto blocks only on human-confirmed tripwires across all tiers
context: A RejectedTripwire carries its own provenance, separate from the rejecting decision. The deck's isVetoBlocker let the dep tier block on confidence >= 0.8 alone (mirroring isStrictBlocker), so a high-confidence LLM-drafted tripwire could fail a build with no human vouch. That makes the 'progressive enforcement / day-one never blocks' DX promise false and reopens the hallucinated-veto risk con_2ce3f2a547-style determinism is meant to avoid.
decision: isVetoBlocker returns true only when the matched tripwire's provenance source includes human_confirmed — for EVERY tier (dep, symbol, pattern). An llm_draft tripwire never blocks regardless of confidence; it only warns (advisory). Confidence ranks the advisory and orders evidence, but never substitutes for the human vouch. One predictable rule: confirmed -> blocks.
consequences: Progressive enforcement is real: a freshly-drafted graph blocks nothing; confirming one tripwire flips the same diff to a strict failure (observable in exit code)
No model output can fail a commit — the block is a set-intersection over a human-vouched record (no prompt-injection / no hallucinated veto)
Mental model is one line a developer can hold: 'it blocks only what I confirmed'
Curation cost is higher than confidence-only blocking, offset by auto-drafting at capture + one-key confirm in hunch review
alternatives_rejected: Let the dep tier block on confidence >= 0.8 without a human vouch (mirrors isStrictBlocker; but an LLM self-score is not a licence to fail a commit, and it breaks day-one-advisory)
Key the gate on the rejecting decision's provenance instead of the tripwire's (a draft tripwire on any high-confidence decision would block — the hallucinated-veto failure)
Make symbol/pattern blockable but keep dep on confidence (inconsistent rule, harder to reason about)
